# pkgbootstrap

pkgbootstrap creates a new R package with an opinionated setup in a
single function call. It combines many `usethis::use_*()` calls, uses
[prek](https://github.com/j178/prek) as pre-commit with
[panache](https://github.com/jolars/panache),
[air](https://github.com/posit-dev/air) and
[jarl](https://github.com/etiennebacher/jarl) hooks.

## Installation

You can install the development version of pkgbootstrap like so:

``` r

# install.packages("pak")
pak::pak("BjarkeHautop/pkgbootstrap")
```

## Example

``` r

library(pkgbootstrap)
bootstrap_pkg("~/GitHub/mynewpkg")

# or with a different license/author
bootstrap_pkg(
  "~/GitHub/mynewpkg",
  author_name = "First Last",
  author_email = "first.last@example.com",
  license = usethis::use_gpl3_license()
)
```

Creating the GitHub repository requires a GitHub token that `usethis`
can find; see
[`usethis::gh_token_help()`](https://usethis.r-lib.org/reference/github-token.html)
if
[`usethis::use_github()`](https://usethis.r-lib.org/reference/use_github.html)
fails.

# Package index

## All functions

- [`bootstrap_pkg()`](https://bjarkehautop.github.io/pkgbootstrap/reference/bootstrap_pkg.md)
  : Create an opinionated R package
